summary.intent: Fix GitHub issue #747 where Gemini (via OpenRouter) rejects tool schemas with `required` entries referencing properties not defined in `properties`, by writing a regression test and implementing a fix.
summary.outcome: Added `PruneOrphanedRequiredTransform` to `sanitize_schema_for_openai_compat()` pipeline, wrote tests covering the non-strict (OpenRouter/Gemini) path, all 25 provider tests pass, and changes were committed and pushed with a PR created.

repo: OpenRouter uses the OpenAI-compat provider with `strict: false`, meaning `patch_schema_for_strict_mode()` is NOT called ā€” orphaned `required` entries survive sanitization.
The `json_schema_ast` canonicalization step resolves orphaned `required` entries by inserting `true` (boolean schema) as their property value, not `{}` as initially assumed.
The schema sanitization pipeline strips keywords like `dependentSchemas`, `if/then/else` that may define properties, leaving orphaned `required` entries ā€” Gemini rejects these.
Existing tests use `panic!` in `let ... else` blocks instead of `.expect()` ā€” clippy enforces this pattern (clippy::expect_used lint).
The project uses a `beads` CLI tool for issue tracking alongside GitHub issues.

code.finding: New `PruneOrphanedRequiredTransform` added to the sanitization pipeline; filters `required` entries where the corresponding property is absent, `{}`, or `true` (canonicalized permissive schema).
New tests cover: direct sanitize_schema_for_openai_compat pruning, and end-to-end to_openai_tools with strict: false (the OpenRouter/Gemini path).

workflow: CUDA-dependent tests fail on macOS ā€” run scoped `cargo test -p providers` rather than the full test suite when working on provider code.
Running `cargo fmt` before clippy avoids formatting noise in lint output.
Canonicalization behavior must be verified empirically (via debug output in tests) rather than assumed ā€” the AST library's output for edge cases like orphaned required entries is non-obvious.

friction: Borrow checker error required collecting property keys before mutable borrow ā€” needed an extra iteration to fix.
Assumed canonicalization produced `{}` for orphaned properties; it actually produces `true` (boolean schema), causing test failures that required debug output inspection to diagnose.
Full test suite fails on macOS due to CUDA dependency ā€” misleading failure unrelated to changes.
open_items: The `true` boolean schema pruning heuristic may be overly aggressive if a legitimate property schema is `true` (accept-all) ā€” worth revisiting if real-world schemas use this intentionally.
OpenRouter/Gemini path has no integration test against a live endpoint ā€” the fix is unit-tested only.
